name: Tiga Credit Card

rewards.displayCategory: UPI, contactless, and Pay-by-3 merchant spends

exclusions: Fuel transactions (surcharge waiver only, no reward points)
additionalBenefits: Limited-period first-year-free offer currently live, excluding GST
Pay by 3: Eligible transactions at select merchants (TATA Cliq, Netmeds, Skull Candy, 1% Club, etc.) auto-converted into 3-month low-cost EMI
Introductory interest rate of 0.99% per month for first 3 months from card issuance
Up to 50 days interest-free credit period on regular purchases
1% fuel surcharge waiver on transactions between ₹500 and ₹3,000 (up to ₹100 per month)
Complimentary air accident cover, credit shield, and lost card liability insurance
additionalDetails: Reward points redeemable on Equitas Xchange portal (xchange.equitasbank.com) for products, vouchers, and travel

salaried: Subject to Equitas Small Finance Bank credit approval
selfEmployed: Subject to Equitas Small Finance Bank credit approval

internalNotes: Card details audited against the live official Equitas Tiga Credit Card page on 2026-06-15.
Card details manually reviewed and verified by user on 2026-06-15.
The live page explicitly shows 3X rewards on UPI, contactless, and Pay-by-3 merchant spends, a 1 RP per ₹100 base earn rate, and 1 RP = ₹0.15 on Xchange redemption.

Unlike Selfe and PowerMiles (which use redemption.pointValueTiers for the Equitas Privilege Program spend ladder), Tiga is kept at a flat 1 RP = ₹0.15: no source publishes a per-tier redemption value ladder for Tiga, and cardinsider states Tiga has no privilege-program enhancement to redemption value. Revisit with pointValueTiers only if the official MITC confirms a Tiga-specific tier ladder.
Pay-by-3 merchants include: TATA Cliq, Tony & Guy, SLAM, Netmeds, Skull Candy, 1% Club, Prashanti Sarees, Wedtree, Lectrix.
The live page explicitly confirms a renewal fee waiver on ₹1.2 lakh annual spends in the previous anniversary year and a limited-period first-year-free offer excluding GST.
Finance charges: 3.75% per month (45% per annum) on revolving balances.
Cash advance fee: 2.5% or ₹250 whichever is higher.
